title: Pilot Sync Pain Question
wikitext:
[]: Pilot Sync Pain Question             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Thu Dec 24, 2009 2:02 pm Throughout the series we see the pilots take injuries based on the battles with the Angels. We all know that what ever damage the Eva takes, the pilots feel a percentage of that damage but what percentage? For example, in the first episode we see Shinji get Sachiels beam through his eye yet his eye is just fine but in EOE Asuka has the lance through the eye and there is blood dripping from it. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by toe mash » Thu Dec 24, 2009 2:21 pm I have a feeling that there isn't a true-absolute-always true answer for that. I think that they feel pain according to how it suits the plot. Like the infamous height thing, or the very unexplained phenomenon of the "1 minute battery under heavy use and otherwise up to 5 minutes" etc. Anyway what I'm getting at is, the amount of pain they feel is inconsistent.. Even though its implied that more synch ratio = more pain, and when its really high they even get injured. But the best thing I can come up with is when the ratio goes up its like at first: no pain at all --> a little pain --> full pain --> actual injury And regarding the eva's themselves feeling pain, unfortunately I'm clueless

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Dec 24, 2009 2:24 pm The Evas probably feel pain, seeing as they're organic lifeforms. The percentage of the damage the pilot experiences increases with the sync rate. Presumably, after a certain percentage, they stop "feeling" the pain, and are actually hurt. This is probably the zone somewhere between 100% and 400%, the higher end being when the pilot loses human form.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Thu Dec 24, 2009 2:30 pm AuraTwilight wrote: The Evas probably feel pain, seeing as they're organic lifeforms. The percentage of the damage the pilot experiences increases with the sync rate. Presumably, after a certain percentage, they stop "feeling" the pain, and are actually hurt. This is probably the zone somewhere between 100% and 400%, the higher end being when the pilot loses human form.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Thu Dec 24, 2009 7:29 pm It’s just like AuraTwilight said, its all dependent on how high the sync rate is. The more closely fused the pilot is with the Eva, the more damage their going to take.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Dec 24, 2009 9:44 pm Of course, the pilots "feel" the injuries and damage to the Evangelion, but are they themselves damaged is the question. Answering such a subjective thing in terms of percentages is a difficult prospect, so we might as well discuss the type and extent of the relationship between pilot and Eva pain/damage. On one level, I think the pilots experience injury as a result of their own involuntary muscle contraction/reactions, etc during synch. This appears to be the case with Shinji in #02 and Rei in #18 at least. However, on another, there appears to be more than simple mental consequences to the pain levels experienced by the pilots. I believe the analogy made at one point was to "jacking in" in the Matrix films. People jacking in could certainly die if the trauma to their "bodies" in the Matrix was high enough, even though their own bodies were undamaged. I think this is similar to the effect that the pilots experience in the Evas. Efforts by control to reduced synchronisations levels in #18 and #19 when the pilots life in in danger suggest that a similar risk is present for Eva pilots. If their Eva bodies "die" then it's likely the pilots could be killed as well. However, what we see in Evangelion probably goes beyond even this. Very strange physical effects on the pilot in the entry plug are seen on several occasions which mimic what is happening to the Eva. In #18, Shinji's neck is shown with actual depressions as a result of the Angel choking the Eva. In #23' both Shinji and Rei experience the same "wrinkling" that their Evas do, and in similar locations. In addition in #25' Asuka's eye and arm are explicitly damaged, and in #26' Shinji has physical wounds from the Eva's "stigmata". I think this is more than just stylisation. What's happening to the Evas is in some very real way being physically experienced by the pilots, probably depending in some way on on the current synch ratio. Given the strange nature of AT Fields and LCL, and particularly given the events of #20, its safe to say that stranger things have happened in the entry plug.

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Thu Dec 24, 2009 10:21 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: However, what we see in Evangelion probably goes beyond even this. Very strange physical effects on the pilot in the entry plug are seen on several occasions which mimic what is happening to the Eva. In #18, Shinji's neck is shown with actual depressions as a result of the Angel choking the Eva. In #23' both Shinji and Rei experience the same "wrinkling" that their Evas do, and in similar locations. In addition in #25' Asuka's eye and arm are explicitly damaged, and in #26' Shinji has physical wounds from the Eva's "stigmata". I think this is more than just stylisation. What's happening to the Evas is in some very real way being physically experienced by the pilots, probably depending in some way on on the current synch ratio.

[]:             Quote Post by User-iel » Fri Jan 01, 2010 2:43 am In general the "blur" of where one organism ends and the other begins is the entire technical purpose of the entry plug system, and the psychological aspect of this is being presented as the operating principle. But that is probably wrong. The mental states of synchronization are an emphasized reaction, perhaps a side effect, of the main action. As the 'contact experiments' demonstrate, the main action present at the core is fusion of realities, or at least the bioinformatic entities present. Since it is also demonstrated that pretty much any cellular form you want can be fabbed using only LCL and information, why wouldn't suitable information be able to re-shape the pilot? So the causality would be injury to EVA > pilot nerve impulses > pilot cognition > active LCL receives the information, and generates the form > pilot injury. Perhaps this is the intended strength of a Dummy System operated EVA as well. No "belief" is being generated by the Dummy, so it might feel pain, but the control being suffers no sympathetic damage.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Fri Jan 01, 2010 8:06 am AuraTwilight wrote: The Evas probably feel pain, seeing as they're organic lifeforms.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Jan 01, 2010 8:11 am Allemann wrote: AuraTwilight wrote: The Evas probably feel pain, seeing as they're organic lifeforms.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Fri Jan 01, 2010 8:22 am Sachi wrote: Allemann wrote: They're dead organic lifeforms. If they ever were alive in the first place.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Jan 01, 2010 8:57 am Allemann wrote: It's obvious from the show. Evangelions are empty organic vessels (meat puppets) whose nervous system is stimulated by a human pilot, or, more precise, his cerebrum where the pilot's mother's soul acts as a conduit between them. Thought, at least in Shinji's case, the soul can perform the pilot function. Evangelions are, literally, walking corpses.

[]: Pilots showing the Eva's wounds             Quote Post by Azathoth » Fri Jan 01, 2010 4:56 pm So I was always under the impression that normally, a pilot feels the pain of any injuries the Eva sustains without actually developing these injuries - when Sachiel breaks Unit 01's arm, Shinji feels as if his arm was broken, even though it's actually undamaged. When the pilot's sync ratio increases - possibly past 100% - they begin to actually manifest these injuries, as Asuka and Shinji both do in End of Eva. However, someone has recently pointed out to me that in all instances of a pilot showing their Eva's wounds, the injury in question was dealt by a Lance of Longinus. Indeed, although Asuka's eye and arm are clearly destroyed by Lances, she does not appear to actually have her guts spilling out when the Series eat Unit 02, despite her presumably unchanged sync ratio. So I ask you two things: 1) Is that correct? Are there any instances in which a pilot shows the wounds of their Eva without a Lance involved? 2) If so, what is it about Lances that gives them the ability to do this?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Jan 01, 2010 5:01 pm Oh, you're in that thread too? First off the top of my head -- Episode 18, when EVA-01 is being strangled, Shinji's neck shows fingermarks.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Fri Jan 01, 2010 5:04 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Oh, you're in that thread too? First off the top of my head -- Episode 18, when EVA-01 is being strangled, Shinji's neck shows fingermarks.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Jan 01, 2010 5:08 pm If the Evas are dead, they can't move. Lrn2biology

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Jan 01, 2010 5:10 pm Since they were so similar, I've merged these two topics (Alos to test the merge mod) There is definitely something strange going on in the entry plugs. We could write off earlier instances of pilot wounding as stylistic, but later incidents in #23' and EoE make a stylistic explanation increasingly untenable

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Fri Jan 01, 2010 5:35 pm You know, upon re-watching a couple of the relevant scenes, it seems that the pilots are either unharmed, or harmed but less severely than the Eva. When Asuka is hit in the face by the Lance, she definitely loses the eye, but she survives. To have a two-pronged spear shoved through your eye into your brain and out the other side would normally kill you, barring ridiculous good luck. In the same way, when Unit 01 is crucified by the Series, Shinji doesn't develop real stigmata; the centers of his palms redden and scar, but they aren't holes going through the hand.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Sun Jan 03, 2010 5:19 pm Sachi wrote: Evangelions may be just meat puppets, but they are still very alive; as much alive as the empty Rei vessels are. Far, far from dead I would argue. All both need to be "normal" functioning organisms is an active soul to control the body's movement . This principal worked with Rei as well as when the Evangelions go berserk; every time the Eva goes Berserk (several cases with Unit-01, two (iirc) with Unit-00, and one partial case with Unit-02) the resident soul awakens and thus gain control of the body. No evidence suggests that they're lifeless organic humps as opposed to soulless vessels like the Rei clones.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Jan 03, 2010 5:52 pm Allemann wrote: Have you properly read what I wrote? That still doesn't change the fact that Evangelions are walking corpses. They don't have their own souls, so obviously, the Lilin invented a complex system of limb control to mimic the role the soul has. If it was your case, they would only need human souls like Yui's for complete control, and no human pilots would be necessary.
